name: Protein Folding (Anfinsen)
description: Protein folding builds higher-order structure from the primary sequence: local secondary structure forms, hydrophobic collapse drives a tertiary fold toward the native free-energy minimum down a folding funnel, subunits assemble into quaternary structure, and misfolded chains are refolded with chaperones to reach the functional native state.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Polypeptide (primary seq)"]
    N2["Local secondary structure"]
    N3["Helices / sheets"]
    N4["Hydrophobic collapse"]
    N5["Tertiary fold"]
    N6{"Native fold reached?"}
    N7["Explore folding funnel"]
    N8["Assemble subunits"]
    N9{"Misfolded / aggregated?"}
    N10["Chaperone refold"]
    N11["Functional native protein"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 -->|re-search| N4
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|yes| N10
    N10 -->|refold| N4
    N9 -->|no| N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Principles that govern the folding of protein chains
The protein-folding problem, 50 years on
